findings.line: Uther was not spectacular. He was clear.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 5 preamble negation; the second sentence carries the meaning cleanly without the negative setup.

findings.line: His voice carried on training, not strength.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 1 direct contrast pivot; the negated second term is scaffolding rather than structural necessity.
findings.suggestedFix: His voice came from training alone.

clusterFlags: Repeated body-part agency in late-cells material
Smart-POV section shows multiple agency-laundering hits

findings.line: For a few precious minutes Ashcombe's case showed itself for what it was: craft.
findings.subject: Ashcombe's case
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas could see that the case was crafted rather than truthful.
findings.reason: KILL — the case is not the agent doing the revealing; the POV character is reading the prosecution's construction.
findings.suggestedFix: For a few precious minutes, Sylvanas could see it for what it was: craft.

findings.line: Her eyes did not come to Sylvanas until the last possible instant, and even then only once.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: Vereesa withheld the look until the last possible instant.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 8 body-part agency; Vereesa is the one choosing when to look.
findings.suggestedFix: She looked at Sylvanas only at the last possible instant, and even then only once.

findings.line: His eyes moved once over the cell, the chains, her face, and sharpened on the bruise at her temple.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: Lor'themar surveyed the cell and fixed on the bruise.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 8 body-part agency; the character, not his eyes, performs the scan and focus.
findings.suggestedFix: He looked once over the cell, the chains, and her face, then fixed on the bruise at her temple.

findings.line: Her mouth nearly found the old answer by reflex.
findings.subject: mouth
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas nearly gave the old answer by reflex.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 8 body-part agency; the mouth is stealing the character's action.
findings.suggestedFix: She nearly gave the old answer by reflex.

findings.line: Years together had taught him enough for that.
findings.subject: Years together
findings.realAgent: Lor'themar had learned enough from years with Sylvanas to read her reaction.
findings.reason: KILL — pattern 6 abstraction-as-teacher; the experience taught him only through what he learned from it.
findings.suggestedFix: He had learned that much over years together.

findings.line: His boots carried the road with them up the corridor toward the cold above.
findings.subject: boots
findings.realAgent: Road dust and the signs of hard travel still clung to him as he went upstairs.
findings.reason: KILL — the inanimate subject is doing metaphorical transport work instead of naming the visible travel residue.
findings.suggestedFix: Road dust still clung to his boots as he headed up the corridor toward the cold above.

audits.cleanReason: The chapter stays inside the hard caps: one corner-of-mouth beat, two tightened uses with one physical use, and four eye/gaze beats that remain within the chapter cap.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A still has more than three MILD KILL findings after revision, driven mainly by repeated body-part agency plus two remaining negation pivots.
